0.q: Two putative MAP kinase genes, ZrHOG1 and ZrHOG2, were cloned from Z. rouxii by their homology with HOG1 from S. cerevisiae. The deduced amino acid sequences of ZrHog1p and ZrHog2p were approximately 93 and 89% homologous to that of Hog1p.

1.q: The HOG1 gene encoding MAPK Hog1p plays an essential role in the osmoregulation of yeast... The MAPK Hog1 phosphorylated through MAPK cascade reaction is translocated into the nucleus and induces up-regulation of the genes required for glycerol biosynthesis.

5.q: When CvNHA1 was expressed in salt-sensitive Saccharomyces cerevisiae cells, increased salt-tolerance was observed, indicating that Cvnha1p possesses an Na+/H+ antiporter function... Cvnha1p participates in the extrusion of intracellular Na+

the ORF of the Z. rouxii Na+-ATPase gene, ZrENA1, was also inserted into the pYES2 vector... The resulting transformants also displayed increased salt tolerance... thought to occur via Na+ extrusion driven by Na+-ATPase.

6.q: Z-SOD2-disruptants of Z. rouxii did not grow in media supplemented with 3 M-NaCl, but grew well in the presence of 50% sorbitol, indicating that the function of Z-SOD2 was closely related to the salt-tolerance of Z. rouxii.

7.q: The gene GPD1 encodes the glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase which controls glycerol synthesis.

8.q: The amino acid identity of Zsod22p was 81% against Zsod2p... Z. rouxii includes at least two copies of the Na+/H+-antiporter gene (ZSOD2 and ZSOD22)

9.q: The amino acid identity of Zsod22p was 81% against Zsod2p... we cloned a second gene (ZSOD22) of the Na+/H+ antiporter from Z. rouxii.

10.q: Na+ extrusion through the cell membrane of Z. rouxii cells is due to the cooperative activities of the cell membrane Na+/H+-antiporter (coded by ZrSOD2) and the cell membrane H+ pump (H+-ATPase)

11.q: In experiments aimed at investigating the physiological function of the DHA pathway in Z. rouxii, we cloned and characterized a DAK gene encoding dihydroxyacetone kinase from Z. rouxii NRRL 2547.

12.q: the Na+/H+-antiporter protein derived from either ZSOD2 or ZSOD22 of Z. rouxii could pump out Na+ ions in S. cerevisiae cells.

13.q: the ORF of the Z. rouxii Na+-ATPase gene, ZrENA1, was also inserted into the pYES2 vector... The resulting transformants also displayed increased salt tolerance... thought to occur via Na+ extrusion driven by Na+-ATPase.

14.q: The HOG1 gene encoding MAPK Hog1p plays an essential role in the osmoregulation of yeast... The MAPK Hog1 phosphorylated through MAPK cascade reaction is translocated into the nucleus and induces up-regulation of the genes required for glycerol biosynthesis.
